div1.entryFree: Bound, vb., to confine, to limit: a gentle flood, who, being stopped, the --ing banks o'erflows, Lucr. 1119. the --ed waters, Troil. I, 3, 111. whose veins b. richer blood than lady Blanch? John II, 431. how are we parked and --ed in a pale, H6A IV, 2, 45. I could be --ed in a nutshell, Hml. II, 2, 260. -- Followed by to: to whose high will we b. our calm contents, R2 V, 2, 38 (or impf. of to bind?). To bound in, in the same sense: glorify the banks that b. them in, John II, 442. this sinister (cheek) --s in my father's (blood) Troil. IV, 5, 129.
